title: salvia azurea var. elata
intro: Salvia azurea var. elata, also known as Blue Sky Painter's Saltbush or Blue Sage, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a variety of the Salvia azurea plant, known for its blue or purple flowers and its traditional use in folk medicine. The medicinal properties of Salvia azurea var. elata include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which are said to provide several health benefits, such as relieving stress, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function.
